content: Autonomous agents can use a HyDE-like hypothetical action generation loop to self-improve their active memory retrieval: when an agent experiences a curiosity-driven open-ended task, it first generates a hypothetical trace of what action it *would* have taken to answer an unvisited knowledge gap, then retrieves relevant past memory traces based on that hypothetical trace, instead of directly searching raw stored experience. This addresses the common problem of misalignment between an agent's current query embedding and stored memory embedding of past relevant experience.
